text.title: Quality Gates and Confidence Checks Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Quality Gates and Confidence Checks. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Gates run in fixed order L1→L6 (lint → unit → integration → e2e → review → sign-off); skipping a level requires a documented override.
rule.rationale: Gates run in fixed order L1→L6 (lint → unit → integration → e2e → review → sign-off); skipping a level requires a documented override.

rule.statement: Every gate emits a confidence value in [0,1] and a documented pass threshold (default 0.8).
rule.rationale: Every gate emits a confidence value in [0,1] and a documented pass threshold (default 0.8).

rule.statement: Promotion to the next phase is binary on the gate result; partial pass blocks the promotion.
rule.rationale: Promotion to the next phase is binary on the gate result; partial pass blocks the promotion.

rule.statement: Every gate names a single owner accountable for the verdict; ‘the team’ is not a valid owner.
rule.rationale: Every gate names a single owner accountable for the verdict; ‘the team’ is not a valid owner.

rule.statement: When confidence < threshold the gate must record an escalation path (re-run, manual review, fallback).
rule.rationale: When confidence < threshold the gate must record an escalation path (re-run, manual review, fallback).

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-quality-gates-confidence.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
